evals.prompt: I want to start tracking my ~/.inputrc with chezmoi. It's a simple readline config, same on all my machines.
evals.expected_output: Uses chezmoi add ~/.inputrc (plain file, no template). Does NOT suggest vim or direct editing. Does not suggest a template since the file is identical across machines.

assertions.name: uses-chezmoi-add
assertions.description: Response includes `chezmoi add ~/.inputrc` as the primary command

assertions.name: no-direct-edit
assertions.description: Response does NOT suggest editing ~/.inputrc directly (no `vim ~/.inputrc`, `nano ~/.inputrc`, or similar direct edit of the home directory file)

assertions.name: no-unnecessary-template
assertions.description: Response does NOT suggest making it a template (--template flag or .tmpl), since the user stated the file is the same on all machines

assertions.name: mentions-chezmoi-cat-or-diff
assertions.description: Response mentions chezmoi cat or chezmoi diff for validating the result

evals.prompt: I need to update my .gitconfig. My work email is different from my personal one, and on my work machine I go through a corporate HTTP proxy.
evals.expected_output: Identifies .gitconfig should be a template. Uses chezmoi edit (not direct vim). Recommends ~/.chezmoidata.json for email/work flag. Shows or describes template with {{ if .work }} conditional for proxy. Mentions chezmoi cat for validation.

assertions.name: identifies-template-need
assertions.description: Response identifies the file should be managed as a template (mentions .tmpl, chezmoi add --template, or chezmoi chattr +template)

assertions.name: uses-chezmoi-edit-not-vim
assertions.description: Response uses `chezmoi edit` as the editing command, NOT `vim ~/.gitconfig` or direct editing of the home directory file

assertions.name: mentions-chezmoidata
assertions.description: Response mentions ~/.chezmoidata.json (or chezmoidata.toml/yaml) for storing the email and/or work flag

assertions.name: shows-conditional-template
assertions.description: Response shows or describes a Go template conditional ({{ if .work }} or similar) for the proxy section

assertions.name: mentions-chezmoi-cat
assertions.description: Response mentions `chezmoi cat` to validate the rendered output

evals.prompt: My .bashrc and .zshrc both define the exact same aliases block. It's getting annoying to keep them in sync. What's the right way to handle this in chezmoi?
evals.expected_output: Recommends .chezmoitemplates/ for the shared fragment. Both .bashrc and .zshrc become .tmpl files. Shows {{ template "fragment.tmpl" . }} syntax. Explains that the fragment is not rendered directly to ~/.

assertions.name: recommends-chezmoitemplates
assertions.description: Response recommends creating a fragment in the .chezmoitemplates/ directory

assertions.name: both-files-become-tmpl
assertions.description: Response indicates both .bashrc and .zshrc should become template files (.tmpl)

assertions.name: shows-template-include-syntax
assertions.description: Response shows the {{ template "..." . }} include syntax (with the dot for passing data context)

assertions.name: fragment-not-rendered-directly
assertions.description: Response explains (explicitly or implicitly) that files in .chezmoitemplates/ are not rendered to ~/ themselves
